q.en: What was Chinzaemon's job, and what made him famous?
q.zh: 琴左卫门是做什么的？又是什么让他出了名？

options.en: He was an armorer; he was famous for making swords that slipped silently into their scabbards
options.zh: 他是一名兵器匠；他因打造能悄无声息滑入剑鞘的剑而出名

options.en: He was a chef known for sweet oatmeal
options.zh: 他是个以甜燕麦片闻名的厨师

options.en: He was a prince who ruled a powerful kingdom
options.zh: 他是一位统治强大王国的王子

options.en: He was a farmer who grew the best rice in the land according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他是个农民，种出全国最好的稻米（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book says Chinzaemon was an armorer whose swords were beautiful and perfectly balanced, and he was even more famous for his scabbards, into which swords 'slipped so easily and silently' that other armorers called him Chinzaemon the Silent.
explain.zh: 书里说琴左卫门是一名兵器匠，他的剑精美且平衡完美；更出名的是他的剑鞘，剑能「轻巧而悄无声息地」滑入其中，所以别的匠人叫他「沉默的琴左卫门」。

q.en: Why did the powerful prince summon Chinzaemon to his court?
q.zh: 那位强大的王子为什么把琴左卫门召到王宫？

options.en: He wanted Chinzaemon to guard the palace gate
options.zh: 他想让琴左卫门看守宫门

options.en: He wanted Chinzaemon to become the prince's cook
options.zh: 他想让琴左卫门当王子的厨师

options.en: He wanted Chinzaemon to tell jokes to make him laugh according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他想让琴左卫门讲笑话逗他笑（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: He wanted Chinzaemon to make weapons for his soldiers and guards
options.zh: 他想让琴左卫门为他的士兵和卫兵打造兵器

explain.en: The prince 'admired the work of the famous silent armorer' and 'wanted Chinzaemon to make weapons for his soldiers and guards.'
explain.zh: 王子「钦佩这位著名的沉默匠人的手艺」，并「想让琴左卫门为他的士兵和卫兵打造兵器」。

q.en: The book is called Chinzaemon the Silent. What does the nickname 'the Silent' refer to?
q.zh: 这本书叫《沉默的琴左卫门》。外号「沉默的」指的是什么？

options.en: His swords slipped into their scabbards so quietly that he seemed silent
options.zh: 他的剑滑入剑鞘时悄无声息，仿佛沉默一般

options.en: He never spoke a single word to anyone
options.zh: 他从不和任何人说一句话

options.en: He was silent because he was very shy
options.zh: 他因为很害羞而沉默

options.en: He fought in complete silence during battles according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他在战斗时全程一言不发（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Other armorers 'began to call him Chinzaemon the Silent' because his swords 'slipped into them [the scabbards] so easily and silently.'
explain.zh: 因为他的剑「轻巧而悄无声息地滑入剑鞘」，别的匠人「开始叫他沉默的琴左卫门」。

q.en: From what point of view is this story told?
q.zh: 这个故事是用什么视角（人称）叙述的？

options.en: First-person — Chinzaemon tells it as 'I'
options.zh: 第一人称——由琴左卫门以「我」的口吻讲述

options.en: Third-person — a narrator describes Chinzaemon's thoughts and actions from outside
options.zh: 第三人称——叙述者从外部描写琴左卫门的想法和行为

options.en: Second-person — the reader is addressed as 'you' according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 第二人称——以「你」来称呼读者（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: It is written as a play script
options.zh: 它是一出剧本的形式

explain.en: The narrator describes Chinzaemon from outside, e.g. 'He thought to himself, "The prince must be a noble man."' — that is third-person.
explain.zh: 叙述者从外部描写琴左卫门，例如「他心想：『王子一定是个高贵的人。』」——这是第三人称。

q.en: How did Chinzaemon play his joke on the sleeping guards?
q.zh: 琴左卫门是怎样捉弄那些打瞌睡的卫兵的？

options.en: He glued their sandals to the ground with strong glue made from tree sap
options.zh: 他用树液制成的强力胶水把他们的草鞋粘在了地上

options.en: He tied their hands together with rope
options.zh: 他用绳子把他们的手绑在一起

options.en: He poured water on their faces to wake them
options.zh: 他往他们脸上泼水把他们弄醒

options.en: He hid their swords so they could not find them according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他藏起他们的剑，让他们找不到（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Using 'very strong glue made from tree sap, he glued their sandals to the ground' so the sleeping guards could not move when the alarm sounded.
explain.zh: 他用「树液制成的强力胶水，把他们的草鞋粘在了地上」，所以警报响起时那些打瞌睡的卫兵动弹不得。

q.en: After the prince grants him anything he wishes, why does Chinzaemon ask to 'sniff into your ear'?
q.zh: 王子答应给他任何想要的东西后，琴左卫门为什么提出「随时凑到你耳边嗅一嗅」？

options.en: He has a bad cold and cannot speak clearly
options.zh: 他得了重感冒，说话不清楚

options.en: It lets him play a prank on the courtiers, who think he is whispering secrets about them
options.zh: 这样他就能捉弄那些廷臣——廷臣以为他在说自己坏话

options.en: He is secretly trying to bite the prince's ear
options.zh: 他偷偷想咬王子的耳朵

options.en: He wants to check if the prince has eaten too much according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他想检查王子是不是吃太多了（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Chinzaemon realized he 'could use this offer to play a big prank on the courtiers' — by sniffing the prince's ear, the courtiers believed he was whispering about them and gave him gifts.
explain.zh: 琴左卫门想到可以「用这个承诺去捉弄王宫里的廷臣」——他凑到王子耳边嗅，廷臣便以为他在说自己坏话，于是送他礼物。

q.en: When the ministers and courtiers saw Chinzaemon sniffing the prince's ear, what did they do and why?
q.zh: 大臣和廷臣看到琴左卫门在王子耳边嗅时，他们怎么做？为什么？

options.en: They reported him to the prince for treason
options.zh: 他们以叛国罪向王子告发他

options.en: They laughed at him for being silly
options.zh: 他们笑他傻

options.en: They gave him gifts, fearing he might whisper bad things about them to the prince
options.zh: 他们送他礼物，生怕他向王子说自己坏话

options.en: They ignored him because they knew it was only a joke according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他们不理他，因为知道那只是个玩笑（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: They 'all began to give Chinzaemon gifts' because they 'thought he was whispering something about them into the prince's ear' and wanted him to recommend them.
explain.zh: 他们「都开始送琴左卫门礼物」，因为「以为他在王子耳边说自己坏话」，想让他替自己美言。

q.en: What was the final result of Chinzaemon's ear-sniffing trick?
q.zh: 琴左卫门「凑耳嗅一嗅」的把戏，最终带来了什么结果？

options.en: He lost his job as the prince's armorer
options.zh: 他丢了王子兵器匠的职位

options.en: He was made to marry the prince's daughter according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他被安排娶了王子的女儿（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: He became very rich — as wealthy as the prince — though he kept living simply
options.zh: 他变得非常富有——富可敌「王」，却依旧过着简朴的生活

options.en: He was thrown in prison for cheating
options.zh: 他因行骗被关进监狱

explain.en: The simple armorer 'was a very rich man' with 'piles of money, beautiful gold jewelry, jade statues' — 'as rich as the prince' — yet 'still lived as he always had.'
explain.zh: 这位朴素的兵器匠「成了大富豪」，有「成堆的钱、精美的金饰、玉雕」——「和王子一样富有」——却「仍像从前一样生活」。

q.en: At the end, the prince makes Chinzaemon his only adviser but sets one condition. What does this show the author values?
q.zh: 结尾，王子让琴左卫门成为自己唯一的顾问，但定下一个条件。这说明了作者看重什么？

options.en: A good adviser should always agree with the prince according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 好的顾问应当永远认同王子（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A trusted friend should be honest and tell the prince the truth, even if it is hard
options.zh: 可信的朋友应当诚实，即使很难也要对王子说真话

options.en: Wealth is the most important thing in life
options.zh: 财富是人生最重要的东西

options.en: Tricks and lies are the best way to get ahead
options.zh: 使诡计和说谎是出人头地的最好办法

explain.en: The only condition was that 'Chinzaemon must tell the prince if he ever acted like a fool' — the story values honest, loyal friendship over flattery and greed.
explain.zh: 唯一的条件是「如果王子做了傻事，琴左卫门必须告诉他」——故事看重的是诚实、忠诚的友谊，而非奉承与贪婪。

q.en: This book is described as 'an expansion and adaptation of a Japanese folktale.' What main message does the story share?
q.zh: 这本书被描述为「日本民间故事的扩写与改编」。它传达的主要寓意是什么？

options.en: Color blindness is a serious problem
options.zh: 色盲是个严重的问题

options.en: Sleeping on the job is the best way to get rich according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 上班时睡觉是致富的最好办法（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Princes should never trust their armorers
options.zh: 王子永远不该信任自己的兵器匠

options.en: Cleverness and loyalty are rewarded, while greed and foolishness are exposed
options.zh: 机智与忠诚会得到回报，而贪婪与愚蠢则会被揭穿

explain.en: The greedy, foolish courtiers are dismissed while the clever, loyal Chinzaemon is rewarded — the folktale teaches that wit and loyalty win out over greed.
explain.zh: 贪婪愚蠢的廷臣被遣散，而机智忠诚的琴左卫门得到奖赏——这个民间故事告诉我们：机智与忠诚终将胜过贪婪。

prompt.en: Chinzaemon used a clever trick (gluing sandals, sniffing the prince's ear) to expose lazy guards and greedy courtiers without hurting anyone. Think of a time you solved a problem with cleverness instead of force. Was your solution fair? What can we learn from Chinzaemon about using wit kindly?
prompt.zh: 琴左卫门用聪明的把戏（粘草鞋、凑耳嗅一嗅）揭穿了偷懒的卫兵和贪婪的廷臣，却没伤害任何人。回想一次你用机智而非蛮力解决困难的经历。你的办法公平吗？从琴左卫门身上，我们能学到怎样「善意地运用智慧」？

en: Chinzaemon glued sleeping guards' sandals with tree-sap glue so they were caught but not harmed (p. 7-9).
By sniffing the prince's ear, he made greedy courtiers reveal themselves and got them dismissed (p. 15-21).
A good answer gives a real example of clever problem-solving and considers whether the trick was fair and kind.
zh: 琴左卫门用树液胶水粘住打瞌睡卫兵的草鞋，让他们被抓却不受伤（第7-9页）。
他凑到王子耳边一嗅，让贪婪的廷臣原形毕露，最终被遣散（第15-21页）。
好的回答会举一个真实「机智解决问题」的例子，并思考这个法子是否公平、善良。
